ABOUT.TITLE: About Us
ABOUT.INTRO_TEXT: , originally founded as TerraBots, is a multidisciplinary robotics initiative born in Peru in 2024 and led by students, graduates, and professors from the
ABOUT.DESC_1: We design and develop autonomous robotic systems for challenging environments, combining robotics, artificial intelligence, embedded systems, mechanical design, and applied research. Our work connects academic excellence with hands-on engineering to create solutions with impact in education, industry, and technological exploration.
ABOUT.DESC_2: More than a technical team, we are a collaborative lab committed to innovation, knowledge transfer, and the formation of future engineers and researchers. Through interdisciplinary work, we aim to contribute to the technological growth of both UNI and Peru.
ABOUT.HISTORY_P1: a group of students from the National University of Engineering (UNI) launched TerraBots, a multidisciplinary initiative focused on building autonomous robotic systems for extreme and demanding environments. What began in classrooms and laboratories quickly grew into a collaborative effort that brought together talent from mechatronics, electronics, systems engineering, industrial engineering, and physics.
ABOUT.HISTORY_P2_PRE: That same year, the team developed
ABOUT.HISTORY_P2_POST: , the first rover platform of the project. It marked the beginning of a hands-on process of research, prototyping, systems integration, and field validation, laying the foundation for future developments.
ABOUT.HISTORY_P3_POST: , a second-generation rover with stronger integration, improved capabilities, and a more mature engineering approach. During this stage, the group also evolved toward a more professional identity under the name
ABOUT.HISTORY_P3_END: , while continuing to preserve TerraBots as its original alias.
ABOUT.HISTORY_P4_PRE: the team participated in the remote phase of the
ABOUT.HISTORY_P4_POST: , marking an important milestone in its international trajectory and demonstrating the potential of young Peruvian talent in robotics and embodied AI.
ABOUT.HISTORY_P5_POST: stands as a platform for innovation, interdisciplinary collaboration, and technological development, with the goal of advancing robotics research and creating real-world impact in Peru and Latin America.
ABOUT.MISSION_TEXT: To develop and advance robotic vehicles through multidisciplinary research and engineering at UNI–FIEE, representing both the National University of Engineering and Peru in high-level international competitions while fostering new generations of students through knowledge transfer in science and technology.
ABOUT.VISION_TEXT: To lead the development of robotics and embodied AI solutions in alignment with global technological trends, contributing to sectors such as mining, agriculture, automation, and related industries while strengthening the technological growth of UNI and Peru.
ABOUT.PILLAR_TECH_DESC: Robotics, AI, CAD, and modern programming.
ABOUT.CTA_DESC: Dive into the advancements, testing, and developments that drive the growth of our autonomous rover. Each project reflects our passion for technological innovation.

NEWS.TITLE: News
NEWS.SUBTITLE: Milestones and recognition from Robotics & Embodied AI Lab — UNI.
NEWS.FEATURED_TITLE: TerraBots Achieves 5th Place Worldwide at the European Rover Challenge 2025
NEWS.FEATURED_P1: TerraBots competed in the Remote Edition of the European Rover Challenge 2025, one of the most demanding international competitions in space and terrestrial robotics. Facing teams from across the globe, the team achieved 5th place worldwide — a milestone that reflects years of interdisciplinary research, engineering rigor, and relentless dedication.
NEWS.FEATURED_P2: This achievement was recognized by the Peruvian Andina News Agency, which published a national feature highlighting the team's contribution to robotics and embodied AI innovation in Peru. The result reinforces the position of Robotics & Embodied AI Lab — UNI as a leading force in Latin American robotics.

PROJECTS.OVERVIEW_DESC: An inside look at the development of Terranaitor — our autonomous rover built for exploration in extreme environments.
PROJECTS.PROCESS_DESC: From strategic alignment to real-world validation, every stage of our engineering workflow is designed to push the boundaries of autonomous robotics.
PROJECTS.PLANNING_DESC: Cross-functional meetings to define research priorities, align engineering objectives, and coordinate milestones across all subsystems.
PROJECTS.ENGINEERING_DESC: Precision mechanics and electronics converge during the design, fabrication, and assembly of each rover iteration.
PROJECTS.VALIDATION_DESC: On-site testing of autonomous navigation, terrain traversal, and sensor integration under real-world conditions.
PROJECTS.SUBSYSTEMS_DESC: Terranaitor is composed of interdisciplinary subsystems that work together to enable autonomous and intelligent operations in challenging environments.
PROJECTS.NAV_DESC: Algorithms for precise autonomous movement in challenging environments. Integrates IMU, GPS RTK, and LiDAR sensors with SLAM-based mapping, route planning, trajectory tracking, and advanced motion controllers for stability and efficiency.
PROJECTS.MECH_DESC: Structural design focused on strength, mobility, and terrain adaptability. Includes CAD modeling, material selection, fabrication, and assembly — plus manipulation systems such as robotic arms and adaptive mechanisms for exploration tasks.
PROJECTS.ELEC_DESC: All electronic hardware including PCB design, power distribution, data acquisition, and communication systems. Ensures reliable connection and operation of sensors, actuators, and embedded modules for field-ready performance.
PROJECTS.AI_DESC: Perception, analysis, and autonomous decision-making through computer vision, machine learning, and neural networks. Enables obstacle detection, object recognition, terrain classification, and intelligent behavior in dynamic scenarios.
PROJECTS.RESULTS_DESC: Key milestones and experimental outcomes from the ongoing development and testing of the Terranaitor rover.
PROJECTS.RESULT1_DESC: Successful obstacle navigation in rough terrain using SLAM and LiDAR integration. Evaluated stability and precision of autonomous movement.
PROJECTS.RESULT2_DESC: Complete assembly of chassis, motors, sensors, battery, robotic arm, and control system — validated for movement, terrain exploration, and sample collection.
PROJECTS.RESULT3_DESC: Neural network model tested for terrain classification and object recognition with on-board camera input during field operations.

PRINTING_3D.TITLE: 3D Printing Service
PRINTING_3D.DESCRIPTION: High-quality 3D printing for prototypes, mechanical parts, custom enclosures, and functional components. We use FDM technology with a variety of materials to bring your designs to life.
PRINTING_3D.FEATURE_1: PLA, PETG, TPU and ABS materials available
PRINTING_3D.FEATURE_2: Custom parts from your STL/3MF files
PRINTING_3D.FEATURE_3: Prototyping for academic and industrial projects
